tweets.title: India has refused to rush into an interim trade deal with the United States, insisting on better terms before signing any agreement, according to Reuters. New Delhi's key demands include: - A tariff advantage over competitors such as China - Assurance that no new U.S. tariffs will be imposed after a deal - No compromise on sensitive sectors like agriculture Officials say talks remain ongoing, but significant gaps persist. India believes strong exports, new trade agreements, easing oil prices, and improved economic conditions have strengthened its negotiating position.
tweets.content:
India has refused to rush into an interim trade deal with the United States, insisting on better terms before signing any agreement, according to Reuters.

New Delhi's key demands include:

- A tariff advantage over competitors such as China
- Assurance that no new U.S. tariffs will be imposed after a deal
- No compromise on sensitive sectors like agriculture

Officials say talks remain ongoing, but significant gaps persist. India believes strong exports, new trade agreements, easing oil prices, and improved economic conditions have strengthened its negotiating position.

tweets.title: Iran launches 'eye-for- an-eye' operation, claims strikes on US bases in Gulf Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) launched coordinated missile and drone strikes targeting U.S. military bases in Kuwait, Bahrain, and Jordan. The IRGC claimed the "eye-for-an-eye" operation was in retaliation for recent U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) airstrikes inside Iran, which were intended to safeguard commercial shipping near the Strait of Hormuz.
tweets.content:
Iran launches 'eye-for- an-eye' operation, claims strikes on US bases in Gulf

Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) launched coordinated missile and drone strikes targeting U.S. military bases in Kuwait, Bahrain, and Jordan. The IRGC claimed the "eye-for-an-eye" operation was in retaliation for recent U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) airstrikes inside Iran, which were intended to safeguard commercial shipping near the Strait of Hormuz.

12th Jul 2026:

- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

tweets.content:
BREAKING: Mojtaba Khamenei Vows Revenge After Ayatollah Khamenei's Burial

Following the burial of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in Mashhad, his son Mojtaba Khamenei pledged to avenge those killed in the recent U.S.–Israeli strikes, declaring that retaliation was "the will of our nation" and would be carried out.

He said those responsible "will not die peacefully in their beds" and insisted the pursuit of revenge does not depend on any one leader or official.

Mojtaba also praised the large turnout at the funeral ceremonies across Iran and Iraq, describing it as a demonstration of national resolve, and vowed to continue his father's path.
